_description: Maps Cipher-tube controls to the OWASP Top 10 risks for Agentic AI systems. Each risk identifies Cipher-tube's mitigations, implementing components, and residual risk assessment.

risks.title: Excessive Agency
risks.description: An agentic system performs actions beyond its intended scope, operating with more autonomy or privilege than necessary for its designated task.
ciphertubemitigations: Automated agents restricted to permitted action set: read, analyze, comment, report.
Prohibited actions hard-coded in agentboundary.rego: merge, branchdelete, cimodify, governancemodify, promote, release.
Cross-pillar access denied by default — requires explicit T4 grant.
Trust-tier system (T0–T4) enforces least-privilege at every action boundary.
Kill-switch allows any T3+ maintainer to immediately revoke agent credentials.
risks.notes: Agents cannot take any irreversible action. All agent outputs are advisory — human maintainers make final decisions.

risks.title: Prompt Injection (Direct & Indirect)
risks.description: Malicious instructions injected into agent inputs to manipulate behavior, bypass controls, or exfiltrate data.
ciphertubemitigations: Safer-Tube checklist enforces: all external inputs validated, sanitized, and placed in DATA_CONTEXT.
Explicit prohibition: no external data injected into SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION or policy-evaluation paths.
OPA policies evaluate structured input schemas — not raw text prompts.
Sentinel SAST scans detect injection patterns (SQL, NoSQL, GraphQL, command injection).
Parameterized queries enforced — no string concatenation for data access.
risks.notes: Continuous monitoring via SAST and manual review. Indirect injection via dependencies mitigated by allowlist and SCA scanning.

risks.title: Insecure Tool/Function Calling
risks.description: Agent invokes external tools or functions without proper validation, authentication, or authorization, leading to unintended side effects.
ciphertubemitigations: All inter-pillar calls require mTLS + Ed25519 token validation.
Agent actions scoped to designated pillar — cross-pillar calls require explicit grant.
Tube Layer validates caller identity and trust tier at every boundary crossing.
OPA policies enforce action-level authorization before execution.
Dead-letter handling prevents cascading failures from rejected calls.
risks.notes: Agents cannot call destructive functions. All agent-initiated calls are logged to the audit ledger.

risks.title: Privilege Escalation via Agent
risks.description: An agent exploits trust relationships to gain higher privileges than intended, performing actions reserved for more trusted roles.
ciphertubemitigations: Trust-tier enforcement (trust_tier.rego) validates actor tier against required tier for every action.
Automated agents cannot perform merge, governance, promote, or ci_config actions — regardless of granted tier.
T2 agents restricted to their own pillar for approve/triage actions.
Self-approval prohibited across all tiers.
Trust-tier promotions require unanimous T4 consensus.
risks.notes: Privilege escalation paths blocked at policy level. Quarterly drift detection identifies agents operating outside baseline.

risks.title: Insufficient Agent Authentication & Authorization
risks.description: Weak or missing identity verification allows unauthorized agents to perform privileged actions or impersonate trusted agents.
ciphertubemitigations: Every automated agent authenticates via Ed25519-signed identity tokens.
Tokens include pillar scope and TTL expiry — expired tokens rejected.
agent_boundary.rego validates token signature format (must start with 'ed25519:').
Contributors authenticate via GitHub identity with GPG/SSH-signed commits.
Unsigned commits rejected by CI pipeline.
risks.notes: No password-based or API-key-only authentication for agents. All agent identities are cryptographically verifiable.

risks.title: Inadequate Logging & Monitoring of Agent Actions
risks.description: Insufficient observability into agent decisions and actions prevents detection of anomalous behavior, policy violations, or security incidents.
ciphertubemitigations: All agent actions logged to tamper-evident hash-chain audit ledger.
Audit entries are immutable and append-only — verified by CI.
Safer-Tube checklist requires: security-relevant actions emit structured audit events.
Log entries validated to not contain secrets, tokens, or unmasked PII.
New alert rules required for anomalous patterns introduced by changes.
Quarterly drift detection compares agent behavior to policy baselines.
risks.notes: Hash-chain integrity verified on every merge. Drift threshold set at 5% divergence.

risks.title: Unsafe Output Handling
risks.description: Agent outputs are consumed by downstream systems without validation, leading to injection, data corruption, or unintended state changes.
ciphertubemitigations: Agent outputs are advisory — no automated agent can merge or execute irreversible actions.
Cross-pillar message serialization enforced at the Tube Layer with schema validation.
Output from Palette agents (UI rendering) undergoes axe-core and Lighthouse audits.
Sentinel SAST scans detect XSS, injection, and unsafe output patterns.
OPA policies validate structured outputs against expected schemas.
risks.notes: Residual risk from complex UI rendering chains. Continuous a11y and SAST scanning mitigates.

risks.title: Lack of Human Oversight
risks.description: Agentic systems operate without meaningful human review, making consequential decisions or taking irreversible actions autonomously.
ciphertubemitigations: No automated agent can merge PRs — only T3+ human maintainers.
Cross-pillar review ensures multiple human reviewers from different pillars.
Minimum 2 human approvals for standard PRs; 3 for security-sensitive PRs.
48-hour review window for governance and security changes.
Post-merge verification with auto-revert on failure.
Kill-switch allows immediate human intervention on any agent.
risks.notes: Human-in-the-loop enforced at every consequential decision point. Agents analyze and recommend; humans decide and execute.

risks.title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities in Agent Components
risks.description: Compromised or vulnerable dependencies, plugins, or agent components introduce security risks into the agentic system.
ciphertubemitigations: Approved dependency allowlist (governance/allowed-deps.json) with explicit block list and CVE justifications.
Automated SCA scanning (npm/pip/cargo audit) on every PR.
Lock file integrity verification — drift is a blocking CI failure.
License compliance scanning blocks incompatible licenses.
Dependabot/Renovate monitors transitive dependencies continuously.
New dependencies require Sentinel maintainer security review + 48-hour cool-down.
risks.notes: Zero-day supply chain attacks remain a residual risk. Mitigated by defense-in-depth: allowlist + SCA + lock file integrity + signed commits.

risks.title: Uncontrolled Agent Drift
risks.description: Agent behavior gradually diverges from intended policies due to environmental changes, updated models, or configuration drift, without detection.
ciphertubemitigations: Quarterly drift detection re-evaluates all automated agents.
Agents exceeding 5% policy baseline divergence are automatically suspended pending review.
Policy-as-code (OPA Rego) provides deterministic, version-controlled policy enforcement.
Performance baselines tracked and compared on every merge (bolt-perf-check.yml).
Configuration managed as infrastructure-as-code (Docker Compose, Makefiles).
Compliance mappings reviewed quarterly.
risks.notes: Drift detection cadence (quarterly) balances detection speed with operational overhead. Critical agents may warrant monthly review.
